topic: An artist's work is displayed without their permission
raw_output:
[story 1]

The gallery lights felt like physical jabs against my skin. I stood by the towering canvas, the vibrant chaos I’d poured my soul into now bathed in an indifferent, sterile glow. A curator, all practiced, smooth gestures, was murmuring about the "unforeseen narrative" of my pieces. My jaw was tight, a knot of pure resistance coiling in my chest. I watched the hushed admiration of the crowd, a silent audience I hadn't invited. "This isn't how I envisioned it," I managed, my voice sounding thin and brittle even to my own ears. I didn't move to adjust a brushstroke or smooth a wrinkle in the fabric; I just held my ground, letting the silence stretch, daring them to challenge the space I occupied.

[story 2]

When I walked into the space, the air immediately thickened with unspoken judgment. The framed portraits of my latest series hung precisely where they were supposed to, every line meticulously cataloged. A man in a sharp suit approached, his expression a practiced mask of polite interest. "Remarkable technique," he said, his tone dripping with a condescension I couldn't help but register. I leaned against a nearby pedestal, crossing my arms so tightly my knuckles bleached white. I looked him straight in the eye, refusing to shrink back. "You have the right to admire," I stated, my voice low and steady, a deliberate counterpoint to his effusive praise. I felt a slow, burning heat rise in my neck, a refusal to be diminished by their approval.

[story 3]

They had hung the collection. The sheer audacity of it made my stomach clench. I surveyed the room—the way the light caught the texture of my work, the way the patrons drifted past, their eyes lingering, analyzing, consuming. I didn't approach the display. Instead, I walked to the edge of the room, my boots clicking sharply on the polished floor, and stared at the wall. A tremor ran through my hand, which I clenched into a fist, the tension vibrating up my arm. *Mine,* the thought echoed, sharp and uncompromising. I took a deep, ragged breath, tasting the stale air, and then turned, walking out without a backward glance, the sound of my departure a decisive finality.
